title: QUIC Connection Runtime Deadline Scheduling Work Item
summary: Keep the connection runtime deadline path traceable while the shard-owned scheduler, generation counters, and timer effect plumbing land.
planned_changes:
- Keep the shard-owned deadline scheduler keyed by monotonic due ticks and stable sequence tie-breakers.
- Keep timer arm and cancel effects generation-aware so stale expirations can be ignored safely.
- Keep the requirement-home tests focused on ordering, stale suppression, and runtime timer expiry handling.
verification_plan: Run the CRT timer tests and the slice-specific dotnet test subset. Keep the proof in the verification artifact and avoid broadening the slice into the RFC 9000 idle-timeout policy math.
completion_notes: Implemented the shard-owned deadline scheduler and generation-aware runtime timer-effect plumbing. The focused `REQ_QUIC_CRT_0045`, `REQ_QUIC_CRT_0047`, `REQ_QUIC_CRT_0048`, `REQ_QUIC_CRT_0049`, `REQ_QUIC_CRT_0050`, `REQ_QUIC_CRT_0051`, `REQ_QUIC_CRT_0052`, `REQ_QUIC_CRT_0053`, `REQ_QUIC_CRT_0054`, and `REQ_QUIC_CRT_0057` requirement-home filter passed 12/12 on 2026-04-30. This closes the scheduler seam only; RFC 9000 idle-timeout value derivation and broader close/drain policy math remain separate.
out_of_scope: RFC 9000 idle-timeout value derivation and PTO floor math beyond the scheduler seam.
Future close/drain policy decisions that are not yet represented in the runtime transition surface.
